is a Turkish professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team from the city of [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"). The team formed in 1967 as the basketball section of [Beşiktaş J.K.](/wiki/Be%C5%9Fikta%C5%9F_J.K. "Beşiktaş J.K."), which was founded in 1903. The team competes in the [Turkish Basketball Super League](/wiki/Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi "Basketbol Süper Ligi").

**Beşiktaş Basketbol**, for sponsorship reasons **Beşiktaş Gain** is a Turkish professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team from the city of [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"). The team formed in 1967 as the basketball section of [Beşiktaş J.K.](/wiki/Be%C5%9Fikta%C5%9F_J.K. "Beşiktaş J.K."), which was founded in 1903. The team competes in the [Turkish Basketball Super League](/wiki/Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi "Basketbol Süper Ligi").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Be%C5%9Fikta%C5%9F_J.K._(men%27s_basketball)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club's men's basketball section started out in the year 1967 and since then, Beşiktaş has played in the first [Turkish Basketball Super League](/wiki/Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi "Basketbol Süper Ligi"), in every season, except in the 1988–89 season, due to their [relegation](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to the [Turkish Basketball First League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_First_League "Turkish Basketball First League") after the 1987–88 season. The club won the Turkish Basketball Super League, under the management of [Ateş Çubukçu](/w/index.php?title=Ate%C5%9F_%C3%87ubuk%C3%A7u&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ateş Çubukçu (page does not exist)") [[tr](https://tr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ate%C5%9F_%C3%87ubuk%C3%A7u "tr:Ateş Çubukçu")] and Tom Davis, in the 1974–75 season.

In October 2010, Beşiktaş announced the biggest transfer in the history of the club's basketball department, with the signing of the former [NBA](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") [Most Valuable Player](/wiki/NBA_MVP "NBA MVP"), [Allen Iverson](/wiki/Allen_Iverson "Allen Iverson"). Iverson signed a 2-year $4 million [net income](/wiki/Net_income "Net income") contract.

In July 2011, [NBA All-Star](/wiki/NBA_All-Star "NBA All-Star") [Deron Williams](/wiki/Deron_Williams "Deron Williams") of the [Brooklyn Nets](/wiki/Brooklyn_Nets "Brooklyn Nets"), announced he would play for Beşiktaş, due to the [2011 NBA lockout](/wiki/2011_NBA_lockout "2011 NBA lockout"). His $5 million net income contract included a clause that would enable him to return to the NBA during the regular season, if an agreement was made between the owners and players association. When the lockout finished, Williams was replaced by [Carlos Arroyo](/wiki/Carlos_Arroyo "Carlos Arroyo").

Arroyo led the team to the [Turkish Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Cup "Turkish Basketball Cup") title in February 2012. In April 2012, the team won the [3rd-tier level](/wiki/European_professional_club_basketball_system "European professional club basketball system") [EuroChallenge](/wiki/EuroChallenge "EuroChallenge") Final against [Elan Chalon](/wiki/Elan_Chalon "Elan Chalon"). After the EuroChallenge championship, the team won the Turkish Basketball Super League Finals against [Anadolu Efes](/wiki/Anadolu_Efes_S.K. "Anadolu Efes S.K."). On September 30, 2012, the team won the [Turkish President's Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_President%27s_Cup "Turkish Basketball President's Cup") against Anadolu Efes.

Starting from the 2023–24 season, the team started to compete in [EuroCup](/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball "EuroCup Basketball"), departing from [Basketball Champions League](/wiki/Basketball_Champions_League "Basketball Champions League"). They advanced to [semifinals](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_EuroCup_Basketball#Semifinals "2023–24 EuroCup Basketball") of the same year's league.

* Beşiktaş Icrypex: 2021–2022[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* Beşiktaş Emlakjet: 2022–2024[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* Beşiktaş Fibabanka: 2024–2025[[8]](#cite_note-8)
